claim_status: Build-readiness closure-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no all-information-ready-for-building claim, no broad M1 implementation claim, no task approval claim, no readiness promotion claim, no developer preview claim, no beta claim, no stable claim, no production claim, no Chrome-class claim, no performance claim, no compatibility claim, no security claim, no accessibility claim, no release readiness claim, no release authority claim, no production authority claim, and no daily-driver claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not name placeholder reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, private contact details, or implied owner authority; a real closure record must replace null fields with owner-reviewed evidence.

unresolved_limitations: Replace with owner-reviewed source-strategy decision evidence.
Replace with owner-reviewed native-shell, page-surface, accessibility, and UI evidence.
Replace with independent fresh-host or approved clean-VM evidence.
Replace with independently reviewed IPC transport and negative-test evidence.
Replace with packaged expected-deny probes and owner-reviewed sandbox policy.
Replace with runner-generated benchmark artifacts and owner-reviewed claim analysis.
Replace with executable profile/session, migration, recovery, and data-loss evidence.
Replace with executable package/update lab and key-separation evidence.
Replace with executed incident tabletop, emergency patch, and role evidence.
Replace with named qualified backup-owner and two-person-control evidence.
Replace with real closure review only after every prerequisite gate is closed or held by approved exception.

gate_axes.required_evidence: Accepted or explicitly rejected ADR-0009 evidence, owner-selected baseline, provenance/equivalence policy, component boundary decisions, JavaScript conflict decision, and public-claim diff.

gate_axes.axis: PB-008 pinned toolchain and PB-009 fresh-host reproduction
gate_axes.required_evidence: Versioned compiler, SDK, linker, Rust, Cargo, Git, shell, and environment manifests plus independent fresh-host or owner-approved clean-VM reproduction with retained bootstrap, doctor, check, xtask, cache, target-directory, source-tree cleanliness evidence, and owner-reviewed toolchain/fresh-host readiness beyond the checked no-claim readiness-review template.

Implemented schema generator, reviewed wire encoding, connection authentication, stale-epoch rejection, timeout and cancellation behavior, negative IPC tests, and owner-reviewed IPC readiness beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template.

Packaged expected-deny probes, effective platform policy capture, failure triage, and owner-reviewed sandbox readiness beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template for renderer, network, storage, GPU, decoder, extension, DevTools, agent, and updater roles.

Approved hardware tiers, clean image/update controls, implemented browser launch runner, raw artifacts, complete competitor pins, equal-workload runs, statistics, owner-reviewed claim bundles, and owner-reviewed benchmark readiness beyond the checked no-claim benchmark readiness-review template.

gate_axes.axis: PB-003 PB-004 PB-005 PB-014 PB-015 native-shell and page-surface
gate_axes.required_evidence: Accepted native-shell, toolkit, page-surface, window/input, accessibility, assistive-technology, resource, latency, and failure evidence before UI or accessibility claims.

Executable profile, Space, session, snapshot, and migration schemas plus disk-full, power-loss, corruption, downgrade, export, deletion, private-session, crash-recovery, protected-work, privacy, data-loss, rollback, and repair tests.
Executable package identity, update metadata, signature, threshold, rollback, migration, crash-loop, privacy-event, update-lab, and production-key-separation evidence.

Private intake tabletop, emergency patch rehearsal, disclosure, backport, signing/update dry run, staged rollout, role, timing, escalation, secret rotation, backup coverage, and incident closure evidence.

Named qualified backup owners, qualification evidence, CODEOWNERS and review-rule reconciliation, stale-access review, ownerless-path review, primary-only-path review, two-person control, and owner-reviewed backup ownership readiness beyond the checked no-claim readiness-review template.
Owner-reviewed implementation kickoff review, dependency graph, documentation-readiness completion audit, closure review, and remaining P0 gate decisions before readiness promotion.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Owner accepts source baseline, dependency/provenance, component boundary, JavaScript, security, maintenance, and public-claim decisions.

Owner accepts the pinned compiler, SDK, linker, Rust/Cargo/Git/shell environment, clean-host equivalence, environment capture, retained logs, source-tree cleanliness evidence, same-host rejection, waiver scope, and toolchain/fresh-host readiness review beyond the checked no-claim readiness-review template.

Owner accepts IPC authority, schema, transport, owner-reviewed IPC readiness beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template, sandbox expected-deny evidence, and owner-reviewed sandbox readiness beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template.

Owner accepts benchmark hardware, corpus, runner, browser pins, raw artifacts, statistics, claim bundles, and owner-reviewed benchmark readiness beyond the checked no-claim benchmark readiness-review template.
Owner accepts toolkit, page-surface, accessibility, input, and resource-budget decisions.
Owner accepts profile/session, package/update, incident-response, migration, rollback, and support implications.
Owner accepts qualified backup coverage, two-person control, and remaining primary-only residual risk.
Owner accepts that first-entry docs, stop/resume, registries, research lanes, task handoff, sequencing, validation, and blockers are current.

release_authority_axes.required_evidence: Human release authority is explicitly granted through production-readiness controls, not from PB-020 template evidence.
Stable signing authority requires separated signing roles, threshold policy, offline root controls, and owner approval.
Update trust changes require owner-approved metadata, keys, rollback, revocation, and supported-version policy.
Supported-version changes require production-readiness support policy, vulnerability SLAs, and release-channel evidence.
Security disclosure authority requires private-process roles, embargo handling, legal/community review, and owner approval.
Irreversible migration approval requires profile/session data-loss, rollback, support, and legal review evidence.
Legal approval requires named qualified human authority and reviewed license, disclosure, notices, privacy, and release materials.
Incident closure requires incident-owner, security, legal/community, support, and release-authority review records.

readiness_lifecycle.required_evidence: Select the concrete closure scope and exact PB/TASK/ADR/RQ records under review.
Collect current source records, machine registries, reports, validators, and raw evidence for every unresolved gate.
Run every focused validator for the affected gates and attach retained output.
Run aggregate validation and preserve output, source-tree cleanliness proof, and failed-command triage.
Confirm proposed TASK records have immutable owner-approved execution manifests before any execution claim.
Confirm all-information-ready-for-building, Chrome-class, performance, compatibility, security, accessibility, production, release, and daily-driver claims have owner-reviewed evidence bundles.
Confirm any exception is explicit, owner-approved, time-bounded, risk-linked, and reflected in support boundaries.
Named owners and independent reviewers accept or reject each gate with evidence.
Update readiness, task, requirement, risk, backlog, crosswalk, owner, review-rule, and production-readiness registries.
Close PB-020 only after every remaining P0 gate is closed or held by explicit approved exception.
Record all unsupported behavior and prohibited claims that remain after review.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim template is cited as a completed closure review.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject PB-020 closure and keep the readiness gate partial.

rejection_rules.condition: Owner reviewer or independent reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and independent review claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Any unresolved required PB gate remains blocked, partial, documented-no-runner, or missing owner-approved exception.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject all-information-ready-for-building and readiness promotion claims.

rejection_rules.condition: ADR-0009, source baseline, provenance, equivalence, component boundary, JavaScript, security, maintenance, or public-claim evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject broad M1 and source-strategy-dependent implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Fresh-host or approved clean-VM reproduction evidence is missing or same-host evidence is overclaimed.

IPC schema, authority, owner-reviewed IPC readiness beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template, negative tests, sandbox expected-deny evidence, or owner-reviewed sandbox readiness beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, production-safety, and hostile-browsing claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Benchmark hardware, corpus, runner, browser pins, raw artifacts, statistics, owner-reviewed claim bundles, or owner-reviewed benchmark readiness beyond the checked no-claim benchmark readiness-review template are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject Chrome-class, performance, memory, energy, and daily-driver claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Native-shell, page-surface, accessibility, assistive-technology, input, IME, or resource evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject UI readiness, accessibility, and everyday product claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Profile/session, package/update, incident-response, migration, rollback, or update-trust evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject production, release, update-safety, data-loss safety, and incident-response claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Backup ownership evidence for named qualified backup owners, two-person control, ownerless-path review, or stale-access review is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner coverage, release authority, signing authority, disclosure authority, legal approval, and incident closure claims.

rejection_rules.condition: A proposed TASK row or no-claim template is cited as execution approval.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject task approval and implementation-completion claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing repository validation is cited as proof of browser behavior, security, compatibility, accessibility, performance, release, or production support.
An exception lacks owner approval, expiry, risk linkage, support-boundary update, or rollback path.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject broad M1 expansion and readiness promotion claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Release, signing, disclosure, legal, incident closure, supported-version, or production authority is inferred from PB-020 evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject authority claim and keep production-readiness gates blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: The closure template is used to say all information is ready for building while any listed blocker remains.
A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, claim boundary, or prohibited claim language without replacement evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-020 partial.
